title: Henriettea tobagensis
taxonomy: Henriettea tobagensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family, also known as the melastome family, comprises approximately 165 genera and over 5,000 species. Henriettea tobagensis is a member of the genus Henriettea, which is native to the Caribbean region. The exact classification of Henriettea tobagensis can be broken down further into its kingdom (Plantae) and phylum (Angiospermophyta). More specific classification details may be found in botanical databases or scientific literature.

intro: Henriettea tobagensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of the Caribbean. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against infections.
